name: Miles Sowden
title: Insurance Executive, Growth and Transformation
credentials: Company Directors Course, Australian Institute of Company Directors (GAICD)
Certificate in Applied Generative AI for Transformations, Massachusetts Institute of Technology (CertAppGenAI)
Graduate Diploma of Applied Finance, Financial Services Institute of Australasia (GDipAppFin)
Bachelor of Business, University of Technology Sydney (BBus)

skills.evidence: GAICD. Presented directly to an entity board (quarterly) and its Risk and Audit Committees (monthly) as the accountable executive across the full spectrum of board topics. Prepared joint papers with the CRO and Chief Actuary. Shaped board decisions through transparent strategic reviews. Presented to boards across three organisations.
skills.headline: GAICD-qualified. Presented quarterly to an entity board across three organisations.

skills.evidence: Led CBA Insurance through the Royal Commission: two witness statements, zero enforceable undertakings, zero fines, zero capital consequences. Signed the ASIC Pricing Promise Review attestation at two separate insurers, neither resulting in regulatory action. Quarterly meetings with ASIC, APRA, AFCA. Engaged Treasury on deferred sales policy; case studies published.
skills.headline: Royal Commission: two witness statements, zero enforceable undertakings, zero fines.

skills.evidence: Presented reinsurance strategy in Singapore, London, and Bermuda (placement up to $1.9bn). Engaged Treasury on policy direction. Negotiated with incumbent insurance providers at Westpac. Managed broker networks at Suncorp and SCI (90% intermediated).
skills.headline: Reinsurance placement up to $1.9bn across Singapore, London, and Bermuda.

skills.evidence: Diagnosed root causes and designed phased transformations at CBA (combined ratio 101.5% to 89.2%), Suncorp (105% to 98%), and SCI (digital availability less than 5% to 60%). Originated the Westpac-Allianz strategic alliance recommendation. Positioned CBA for divestment at 1.8x PCA with a three-year funded roadmap.
skills.headline: Combined ratio from 101.5% to 89.2% at CBA. Turnarounds delivered at Suncorp and SCI.

skills.evidence: Deployed agentic AI for underwriting at 97% accuracy (SCI). AI tools to 20% of technical users. Built AI governance framework. Digital sales from 20% to 70% at CBA. End-to-end digital underwriting with real-time pricing at SCI. Qantas workforce planning model for catastrophe surge at CBA.
skills.headline: Agentic AI at 97% underwriting accuracy. Digital sales from 20% to 70% at CBA.

skills.evidence: Led CBA Insurance through divestment to Hollard: joint strategy, funded roadmap, and separation of a $1bn+ portfolio deeply integrated into the Group. Delivered the Suncorp/Promina post-merger integration for intermediated personal lines, consolidating platforms and teams across two legacy businesses. Ran six-month competitive process at Westpac, shortlisting six insurers to two; Allianz selected as strategic alliance partner with portfolio transfer.
skills.headline: Led CBA Insurance divestment to Hollard. Integrated Suncorp/Promina intermediated personal lines post-merger.

skills.evidence: Delivered first underwriting profit at Suncorp personal lines after four consecutive loss years (105% to 98%). Full P&L and balance sheet accountability at CBA including reinsurance placement up to $1.9bn. Built risk cultures and control frameworks that effectively delivered regulatory reforms across multiple institutions. Developed novel rating factor reducing premiums 10% while improving loss ratio 1%. Identified and removed inappropriate rating factors from the CBA pricing model.
skills.headline: First underwriting profit at Suncorp personal lines after four consecutive loss years.

skills.evidence: Digital availability from less than 5% to 60% at SCI. Digital sales from 20% to 70% at CBA with headcount held flat. Quote-to-sale conversion 20% to 50% in CBA home buying channel. End-to-end digital underwriting with real-time pricing at SCI. Broker portal redesign with RPA and API integrations eliminating double-keying.
skills.headline: Digital availability from less than 5% to 60% at SCI. Quote-to-sale conversion doubled at CBA.

skills.evidence: Full P&L and balance sheet accountability for CBA's $1bn+ insurer. Combined ratio 101.5% to 89.2%. Motor profitable within 15 months (never previously profitable). Reinsurance placement up to $1.9bn. Divestment at 1.8x PCA. Portfolio growth $115m to $200m at Westpac.
skills.headline: Full P&L and balance sheet for CBA's $1bn+ insurer. Motor profitable within 15 months.

skills.evidence: Cleared 30,000-task backlog at SCI in two months with unchanged resourcing. Underwriting cycle time reduced 19%. Qantas workforce planning model for catastrophe surge at CBA. Tiered supplier model (outcomes, cost, timeframes, rework rates) for claims. RPA for billing, admin, data entry, correspondence. Headcount held flat across CBA transformation.
skills.headline: 30,000-task backlog cleared in two months with unchanged resourcing at SCI.

skills.evidence: Staff engagement 50% to 70% at CBA (during Royal Commission, pandemic, divestment). 66% to 92% at Westpac (during outsourcing programme). 76% at SCI. Built succession at SCI where internal promotion led entire operations team after external hire departed.
skills.headline: Staff engagement 50% to 70% at CBA during Royal Commission, pandemic, and divestment.

skills.evidence: NPS from +15 to +45 at CBA, +25 to +44 at Westpac. CANSTAR Outstanding Value five consecutive years. Novel rating factor reduced premiums 10%, improved loss ratio 1%, no adverse rating on lower economic groups. Significant customer remediations refunded proactively at CBA. Proactive outreach to entitled customers at Westpac.
skills.headline: NPS from +15 to +45 at CBA. CANSTAR Outstanding Value five consecutive years.

skills.evidence: Designed 10-person executive team at CBA using four composition levers: external hire, internal promotion, cross-functional moves, exits. Team stayed in place after divestment. Delegated business accountability during Royal Commission. Weekly, monthly, quarterly operating rhythm. Direct reports presented to the entity board.
skills.headline: Built 10-person executive team at CBA. Every one stayed in place through divestment.

caseStudies.title: Building a direct insurer within a broker-dominated business
caseStudies.role: General Manager, Data and Technology Solutions
caseStudies.summary: Built data, technology and AI capabilities for a specialist strata MGA. Sequenced operations, digital, then AI. Digital availability from less than 5% to 60%. Agentic AI at 97% accuracy.
outcomes: Digital availability from less than 5% to 60%
30,000-task backlog cleared in two months, unchanged resourcing
Underwriting cycle time reduced 19%
Agentic AI deployed at 97% accuracy
Staff engagement 76%
Absorbed doubled call volumes and 10x web traffic from competitor crisis with improved service levels
Strong leadership succession in place

caseStudies.title: Transforming a structurally broken $1 billion insurer
caseStudies.summary: Full P&L accountability for $1bn+ GWP, 700 staff, one million customers. Combined ratio from 101.5% to 89.2%. Motor profitable within 15 months, a first for the book. Digital sales from 20% to 70%. Navigated the Royal Commission without material consequences.
outcomes: Combined ratio from 101.5% to 89.2% average pre-divestment
Motor profitable within 15 months (never previously profitable)
NPS from +15 to +45 within two years
Staff engagement from 50% to 70% within two years
Digital sales from 20% to 70%, headcount held flat
Customer remediations refunded proactively
CANSTAR Outstanding Value five consecutive years
Royal Commission: zero enforceable undertakings, zero fines, zero capital consequences
ASIC Pricing Promise Review signed on time, no regulatory action
Reinsurance placement up to $1.9bn
Divestment at 1.8x PCA with funded roadmap

caseStudies.title: Portfolio transition and regulatory closure
caseStudies.summary: Signed the ASIC Pricing Promise Review attestation at a second insurer. Entered the Cyclone Reinsurance Pool. Nine-month tenure confirmed the importance of alignment between executive and board on transformation appetite.
outcomes: ASIC Pricing Promise Review signed on time, no regulatory action
Cyclone Reinsurance Pool entered
Enduring board relationships maintained

caseStudies.title: First underwriting profit after four consecutive loss years
caseStudies.summary: Delivered the first underwriting profit in Vero intermediated personal lines after four consecutive years of losses. Combined ratio from 105% to 98%. Post-merger platform integration across $2bn GWP. HNW packaged product to market in six months.
outcomes: Combined ratio from 105% to 98% (first underwriting profit after four consecutive loss years)
HNW packaged product to market in six months, 1.4-year payback
Platform integration across $2bn GWP (best-of-both approach)
Broker network rationalised to profitable relationships
Three wordings consolidated to one
Portfolio handed over in good shape

caseStudies.title: Growing a portfolio and originating a strategic alliance
caseStudies.summary: Grew the insurance portfolio from $115m to $200m. Originated the Westpac-Allianz strategic alliance. Ran a six-month competitive process. NPS from +25 to +44. Staff engagement from 66% to 92%.
outcomes: Portfolio grown from $115m to $200m
NPS from +25 to +44
Staff engagement from 66% to 92% over 20 months
Allianz selected as strategic alliance partner
Distribution strategy and operating model delivered
ASIC confirmed products were defensible

philosophy: Pragmatic outcomes for users come first. Technology sits safely and securely in the background, powering the experience without being the experience. Build the strategy, teams and culture that deliver lasting performance. Evidence over adjectives.
